output.summary: Fix missing spacing between filters and cards in the editor (TSX) preview. The FilteredServerSideRender component extracts the innerHTML of .archive-inner, losing the element itself and its classes (grid grid-cols-1 gap-gap-4). The wrapper <div> rendered by FilteredServerSideRender has no className, so the gap-gap-4 spacing between the filter bar and the results grid is absent in the editor. Fix by passing className='archive-inner grid grid-cols-1 gap-gap-4' to FilteredServerSideRender, matching the pattern used by TestimonialsCarousel, DynamicContentCarousel, and Form blocks.

fileChanges.description: Add className prop to FilteredServerSideRender to replicate the .archive-inner element's classes
fileChanges.details:
Line 161: Change: To: Rationale: The FilteredServerSideRender component (from @taktdev/components) works by: 1. Rendering the full PHP output in a hidden container 2. Finding the element matching querySelector ('.archive-inner') 3. Extracting that element's innerHTML (not outerHTML) -- line 82 of the component source 4. Rendering the innerHTML inside a
wrapper with the className prop applied Because innerHTML is used, the .archive-inner element itself (and its classes) are stripped. The className prop on FilteredServerSideRender is passed through to the wrapper
(line 167 of the component source), so adding 'archive-inner grid grid-cols-1 gap-gap-4' recreates the same structure. The PHP frontend has:
...
...
Without className, the editor renders:
...
...
With className, the editor renders:
...
...
This follows the established pattern in other blocks: - TestimonialsCarousel: className="swiper-parent overflow-hidden relative" - DynamicContentCarousel: className="swiper-parent overflow-hidden grid grid-cols-1 grid-rows-1" - Form: className="form-section-form"

designDecisions.question: Should the className include the 'archive-inner' class name in addition to the layout utilities?
designDecisions.recommendation: Yes. Include 'archive-inner' for two reasons: (1) it maintains consistency with the PHP markup so any CSS rules targeting .archive-inner apply in both contexts, (2) it follows the convention seen in other blocks (e.g., Form uses className='form-section-form', TestimonialsCarousel uses className='swiper-parent ...'). The full className should be 'archive-inner grid grid-cols-1 gap-gap-4' to exactly match the PHP element.
